description: Train two LoRA world-model adapters on a shared frozen sub-2B base: wm_low (primitive edits) and wm_high (macro edits), with a cycle-consistency loss L_cycle and status/error prediction heads. Intrinsic eval: next-state exact-match, status-F1, error_class accuracy, k-step rollout fidelity.
motivation: Test the MVP's core falsifiable claim - is Lean dynamics learnable at T4 scale?
formal_results: Planned - not run. src/wm/ is an empty stub; COMPUTE_LEDGER.md records 0 T4-GPU-h.
results_description: No model trained; no intrinsic-eval numbers.
conclusions: Hypothesis: a sub-2B model can learn Lean dynamics well enough to be a useful surrogate. Untested.
